title: AI Can Learn Scientific Taste
task: Learning scientific taste for high-impact research idea generation
key_results: Scientific Judge outperforms GPT-5.2 and Gemini 3 Pro on citation-prediction; Scientific Thinker generates research ideas with higher potential impact than baselines
comments: Novel application of preference modeling to scientific impact prediction, though citation count as ground truth may not fully capture true impact.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI can learn scientific taste via community feedback signals
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Scientific Judge generalizes to future years, unseen fields, and peer-review preferences

title: HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions
task: Physics-grounded 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions from casual video
key_results: First stable, simulation-ready HSI reconstructions; direct deployment to real-world humanoid robots
comments: Strong physics-in-the-loop approach bridging perception-simulation gap, though limited to lab-scale validation.

outputs: stable 3D human pose and scene geometry

hypotheses.hypothesis: Treating physics simulator as active supervisor improves stability and simulation readiness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Produces first stable HSI reconstructions deployable to real robots

title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
task: Evaluating agentic planning and tool use in realistic enterprise environments
key_results: Claude Opus 4.5 achieves only 37.4% on 1,150 tasks; oracle plans improve performance by 14-35 pp; agents fail to refuse infeasible tasks 46.1% of the time
comments: Valuable benchmark exposing critical safety gaps; strategic reasoning identified as primary bottleneck, suggesting current agents are premature for autonomous enterprise deployment.

inputs: enterprise task descriptions, database schema, 512 functional tools
outputs: tool calls, database queries, task completion

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strategic reasoning is the primary bottleneck in enterprise agentic planning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: oracle plans improve performance by 14-35 percentage points

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current agents are ready for autonomous enterprise deployment
hypotheses.result: Refuted: top model achieves only 37.4% success; agents frequently fail to refuse infeasible tasks

title: OpenSeeker: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
task: Open-source frontier search agent development with synthetic training data
key_results: 11.7k synthesized samples achieve SOTA on BrowseComp (29.5% vs 15.3%), BrowseComp-ZH (48.4% vs 46.7% Tongyi), outperforming larger industrial systems
comments: Impressive data efficiency and full open-sourcing democratizes research; synthetic trajectory refinement is key innovation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-quality synthetic data can match industrial search agent performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 11.7k samples achieve SOTA, surpassing much larger proprietary systems

title: Grounding World Simulation Models in a Real-World Metropolis
task: City-scale autoregressive video generation grounded in real Seoul streets
key_results: Generates spatially faithful, temporally consistent videos over 100+ meter trajectories; outperforms recent video world models on three cities
comments: Novel retrieval-augmented conditioning approach with cross-temporal pairing is elegant; evaluation limited to urban street scenes.

inputs: street-view image conditioning, camera trajectory, optional text prompts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Retrieval-augmented conditioning enables realistic city-scale video grounding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: generates spatially faithful long-horizon videos matching real urban environments

title: Attention Residuals
task: Efficient residual connection design for deep LLMs via learned depth-wise aggregation
key_results: Consistent improvements across model sizes; Block AttnRes integrates into 48B-param Kimi with 1.4T token pre-training; more uniform gradients across depth
comments: Principled approach to addressing PreNorm dilution with minimal overhead; practical for large-scale training.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Softmax attention over layer outputs mitigates PreNorm dilution better than uniform accumulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: more uniform output magnitudes and gradient distribution; downstream improvements across all tasks

title: Mixture-of-Depths Attention
task: Attending over both current and preceding layer KV pairs to preserve features in deep LLMs
key_results: 0.2 perplexity improvement across 10 validation benchmarks; 2.11% downstream task improvement; 97.3% FlashAttention-2 efficiency at 64K length
comments: Hardware-efficient solution to signal degradation in depth scaling; combines MoDA with post-norm for best results.

inputs: sequence KV pairs at current layer, depth KV from preceding layers
outputs: attended features with preserved shallow-layer signals

hypotheses.hypothesis: Attention heads can selectively preserve features from earlier layers to combat signal dilution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: consistent perplexity and downstream improvements; post-norm combination yields best results

title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
task: Lossless distillation of transformer LLMs into xLSTM linearized architectures
key_results: xLSTM students recover most teacher performance, exceeding on some tasks; effective across Llama, Qwen, Olmo families
comments: Merging stage for linearized experts is novel; achieves energy-efficient replacements but with task-dependent trade-offs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: xLSTM students can achieve lossless distillation via merging stage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: recover most teacher performance and exceed on some downstream tasks

title: Anatomy of a Lie: A Multi-Stage Diagnostic Framework for Tracing Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
task: Diagnosing hallucinations in VLMs via geometric-information duality in cognitive state space
key_results: SOTA hallucination detection across POPE, MME, MS-COCO; 96% agreement with human preferences; identifies three pathological states (perceptual instability, logical-causal failure, decisional ambiguity)
comments: Innovative information-theoretic framework linking geometry to surprisal; enables causal attribution of failures.

outputs: cognitive state space projection, hallucination detection, causal pathology attribution

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometric abnormality in cognitive state space is equivalent to information-theoretic surprisal
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: geometric-information duality principle holds; enables efficient hallucination detection

title: ViFeEdit: A Video-Free Tuner of Your Video Diffusion Transformer
task: Video-free tuning of video diffusion transformers using only 2D image data
key_results: Versatile controllable video generation and editing without video training data; maintains temporal consistency with minimal parameters
comments: Clever decoupling of spatial and temporal attention via reparameterization; practical for data-scarce scenarios.

outputs: controllable video generation/editing with temporal consistency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Architectural reparameterization can decouple spatial and temporal attention for efficient video tuning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: achieves temporal consistency while training only on 2D images with minimal parameters

title: Safe and Scalable Web Agent Learning via Recreated Websites
task: Training web agents safely via automatically cloned verifiable synthetic websites
key_results: Agents generalize to unseen websites, achieve site-specific mastery via self-evolution, benefit from scaling environments
comments: Elegant solution treating LMs as environment creators; deterministic verifiable rewards eliminate heuristic judges.

model_io.model: Web agent (trained in VeriEnv synthetic environments)
outputs: web actions with deterministic verifiable rewards

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents trained on synthetic cloned websites generalize to real websites
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: generalize to unseen websites and self-evolve through environment expansion

title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
task: Stochastic generative optimization of complex systems via LLM-guided exploration
key_results: POLCA converges to near-optimal solutions under stochasticity; SOTA on τ-bench, HotpotQA, VeriBench, KernelBench
comments: Principled framework handling optimization stochasticity; priority queue and LLM summarizer enable efficient search.

inputs: numerical rewards, text feedback, candidate solutions

hypotheses.hypothesis: POLCA converges to near-optimal solutions under stochasticity with priority queuing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: theoretical convergence proof; SOTA across diverse benchmarks

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
task: Predicting and learning optimal early-stopping points in chain-of-thought reasoning
key_results: 14%-55% CoT length reduction while maintaining or outperforming performance on MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval, GPQA
comments: Pragmatic approach leveraging first answer arrival; significant compute savings for reasoning models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: First answer arrival in CoT is predictable and indicates optimal reasoning length
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 14%-55% length reduction achievable with performance parity

title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics
task: Evaluating multimodal LLMs on webpage recreation fidelity from demonstration videos
key_results: 19 models evaluated; gaps in fine-grained style and motion quality; rubric-based evaluation achieves 96% human agreement
comments: First benchmark for video-conditioned webpage generation; 175 synthetic webpages ensure reproducibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video-conditioned generation enables faithful webpage recreation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: videos convey interaction flow better than static inputs; human-aligned rubric at 96% agreement

title: Make it SING: Analyzing Semantic Invariants in Classifiers
task: Interpreting classifier invariants and semantic content via null-space geometry
key_results: ResNet50 leaks semantic attributes to null space; DinoViT better maintains class semantics; provides natural language descriptions of invariant variations
comments: Novel application of multimodal VLMs to interpret classifier geometry; reveals important semantic leakage in standard models.

inputs: classifier, input image or image set
outputs: equivalent images, natural language semantic descriptions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Vision-language models can interpret semantic content of classifier null-space invariants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: provides human-interpretable descriptions; reveals model differences (ResNet vs DinoViT)

title: Motivation in Large Language Models
task: Examining whether LLMs exhibit motivation-like constructs affecting behavior
key_results: Self-reported motivation aligns with behavioral signatures, varies across task types, can be modulated by external factors; mirrors human psychology
comments: Intriguing psychological perspective on LLM behavior; raises questions about anthropomorphization vs genuine information structure.

model_io.model: LLMs (queried for motivation and task behavior)

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs exhibit motivation-like constructs organizing behavior similarly to human psychology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: motivation reports correlate with performance, effort, and choice patterns

title: Understanding Reasoning in LLMs through Strategic Information Allocation under Uncertainty
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms via epistemic verbalization and information-theoretic decomposition
key_results: Epistemic verbalization (uncertainty externalization) drives reasoning performance over surface tokens; enables information acquisition and sufficiency
comments: Principled information-theoretic framework unifying Aha moment phenomena; emphasizes uncertainty over specific token patterns.

outputs: reasoning trajectory with procedural and epistemic components

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uncertainty externalization (epistemic verbalization) is critical for reasoning, not specific surface tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strong reasoning driven by externalized uncertainty; unifies prior Aha moment findings

title: EvoClaw: Evaluating AI Agents on Continuous Software Evolution
task: Evaluating AI agents on continuous long-term software evolution with technical debt
key_results: 12 frontier models drop from >80% isolated task performance to ≤38% in continuous settings; exposes profound struggles with error propagation
comments: Important benchmark revealing critical gap; most agents fail at long-term maintenance despite strong one-off capabilities.

outputs: code generation maintaining system integrity over time

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents capable on isolated tasks fail on continuous software evolution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 40-50 percentage point performance drop in continuous vs isolated settings

title: Code-A1: Adversarial Evolving of Code LLM and Test LLM via Reinforcement Learning
task: Adversarial co-evolution of code and test generation LLMs via competing RL objectives
key_results: Code-A1 matches or exceeds performance of models trained on human-annotated tests; eliminates self-collusion in self-play; improves test generation
comments: Elegant solution to self-play dilemma via architectural separation; Mistake Book mechanism for experience replay is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adversarial code-test co-evolution avoids self-collusion and generates higher-quality tests
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: matches human-test performance; Test LLM generates targeted adversarial tests

title: The PokeAgent Challenge: Competitive and Long-Context Learning at Scale
task: Large-scale competitive and long-context decision-making via Pokemon battles and speedrunning
key_results: 100+ competition teams; 20M+ battle trajectories; shows orthogonality between Pokemon and standard LLM benchmarks; considerable gaps to human elite play
comments: High-quality living benchmark; partial observability and long-horizon planning remain open problems.

model_io.model: RL baselines, LLM-based agents, heuristic agents

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pokemon benchmarks measure orthogonal capabilities to standard LLM evaluation suites
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: BenchPress analysis shows near-orthogonality; unsolved benchmark for future research

title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
task: Affordance prediction from 360-degree panoramic imagery with coarse-to-fine visual routing
key_results: PAP outperforms SOTA methods on ultra-high-resolution panoramic images; existing affordance methods fail on panoramic vision; introduces PAP-12K benchmark with 12k QA pairs
comments: Novel panoramic affordance prediction task; foveal visual system inspiration is elegant for handling extreme resolution.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Panoramic imagery improves affordance prediction via holistic context
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: existing methods fail on panoramic vision; PAP handles ultra-high resolution effectively

title: Riemannian Motion Generation: A Unified Framework for Human Motion Representation and Generation via Riemannian Flow Matching
task: Human motion generation via Riemannian manifold representation and flow matching
key_results: SOTA FID on HumanML3D (0.043); state-of-the-art on MotionStreamer format; FID 5.6, R@1 0.86 on MotionMillion
comments: Principled geometry-aware approach; scale-free representation with intrinsic normalization is theoretically motivated.

outputs: human motion sequences on product manifold

hypotheses.hypothesis: Riemannian manifold representation captures motion geometry better than Euclidean space
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: T+R (translation+rotations) factorization most stable; SOTA FID and metrics

title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
task: Evaluating multimodal LLMs on omni-modal understanding and reasoning in long complex videos
key_results: Best closed-source model (64.2%), best open-source (46.8%); substantial performance gaps on 13 skill categories; frequent failures in long-form understanding
comments: Valuable benchmark exposing multimodal reasoning gaps; 15k questions on 9k videos provide comprehensive evaluation.

inputs: long complex videos with visual, audio, textual signals

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs can effectively integrate omni-modal signals in long videos
hypotheses.result: Refuted: substantial gaps; best model reaches 64.2% accuracy; models fail on fundamental skills in long-form settings

title: Supervised Fine-Tuning versus Reinforcement Learning: A Study of Post-Training Methods for Large Language Models
task: Comprehensive analysis of SFT vs RL post-training paradigms for LLMs
key_results: Unified framework showing SFT and RL are interconnected; identifies hybrid paradigms as emerging trend; clarifies when each method is effective
comments: Thorough synthesis covering 2023-2025 literature; provides practical guidance for practitioners.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SFT and RL are distinct but complementary post-training paradigms
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: unified framework shows interconnection; hybrid methods outperform single-paradigm approaches

title: FineRMoE: Dimension Expansion for Finer-Grained Expert with Its Upcycling Approach
task: Fine-grained MoE design extending to both intermediate and output dimensions with upcycling
key_results: 6x parameter efficiency vs baseline; 281x lower prefill latency; 136x higher decoding throughput; SOTA across 10 benchmarks
comments: Principled scaling beyond single-dimension constraints; upcycling from existing models is cost-effective.

outputs: model predictions via sparse expert routing

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extending fine-grained design to output dimension improves efficiency and performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 6x parameter efficiency, significant latency/throughput improvements across benchmarks

title: Learning Latent Proxies for Controllable Single-Image Relighting
task: Controllable single-image relighting via physical priors and latent proxy learning
key_results: Up to +2.4 dB PSNR and 35% lower RMSE under controlled lighting shifts; photometrically faithful with accurate continuous control
comments: Efficient design avoiding full intrinsic decomposition; sparse physical cues sufficient for guidance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparse physical priors enable controllable relighting without full intrinsic decomposition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: achieves photometrically faithful results with efficient latent proxy learning

title: Training-free Detection of Generated Videos via Spatial-Temporal Likelihoods
task: Training-free detection of synthetic videos via spatial-temporal likelihood scoring
key_results: STALL outperforms image and video-based baselines on public benchmarks and new ComGenVid; zero-shot, model-agnostic detection
comments: Theoretically justified likelihood-based approach; avoids synthetic training data while achieving strong generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Training-free likelihood scoring detects synthetic videos via spatial-temporal anomalies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms baselines without access to generator models or synthetic data

title: RS-WorldModel: a Unified Model for Remote Sensing Understanding and Future Sense Forecasting
task: Remote sensing world model for spatiotemporal change understanding and future forecasting
key_results: FID 43.13 on text-guided forecasting; outperforms open-source models 120x larger; surpasses Gemini 2.5 on change QA with 2B parameters
comments: Efficient unified model; three-stage training including geographic pre-training is well-designed.

outputs: change understanding answers, future scene forecasts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified world model with geographic pre-training improves remote sensing tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 2B model outperforms 120x larger baselines; achieves SOTA FID on forecasting

title: HorizonMath: Measuring AI Progress Toward Mathematical Discovery with Automatic Verification
task: Evaluating AI progress on predominantly unsolved mathematical problems with automatic verification
key_results: GPT 5.4 Pro proposes solutions improving on best-known results for 2 problems; most models score near 0%; immune to data contamination
comments: Novel benchmark design with hard discovery but easy verification; enables growth as community solves problems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI models can make progress on unsolved mathematical problems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: GPT 5.4 Pro solves 2 problems improving on published results; most models near 0%

title: When Does Sparsity Mitigate the Curse of Depth in LLMs
task: Understanding sparsity's role in mitigating curse of depth and improving layer utilization in LLMs
key_results: Sparsity improves layer utilization by reducing variance; 4.6% downstream accuracy improvement from depth-scaling recipe
comments: Principled analysis connecting sparsity to variance control; practical guidance for deep model training.

model_io.model: LLMs with various sparsity types (weight, attention, expert)
outputs: layer utilization metrics, downstream task performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparsity mitigates curse of depth by regulating variance propagation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: consistent relationship across implicit and explicit sparsity; 4.6% improvement on downstream tasks

title: VisionCoach: Reinforcing Grounded Video Reasoning via Visual-Perception Prompting
task: Improving spatio-temporal grounding in video reasoning via adaptive visual prompting and RL
key_results: SOTA on V-STAR, VideoMME, World-Sense, VideoMMU, PerceptionTest, Charades-STA; inference-time efficiency without external tools
comments: Clever training-time visual prompting with self-distillation for inference; addresses grounding without tool overhead.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Visual prompting during RL training enables grounded reasoning internalized for inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SOTA across benchmarks; single efficient inference path without external perception

title: FlashMotion: Few-Step Controllable Video Generation with Trajectory Guidance
task: Few-step trajectory-controllable video generation via distillation and hybrid training
key_results: FlashMotion achieves both video quality and trajectory accuracy with few steps; new FlashBench evaluates long-sequence controllable generation
comments: Practical acceleration while maintaining trajectory control; hybrid diffusion-adversarial finetuning is effective.

outputs: trajectory-accurate videos in few denoising steps

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory adapters can maintain control fidelity after distillation via hybrid finetuning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: surpasses video distillation methods; achieves quality and trajectory accuracy

title: GlyphPrinter: Region-Grouped Direct Preference Optimization for Glyph-Accurate Visual Text Rendering
task: Region-level glyph accuracy optimization in text rendering via preference-based learning
key_results: GlyphPrinter outperforms existing methods; Region-GDPO substantially enhances glyph accuracy; Regional Reward Guidance enables controllable precision
comments: Novel region-grouped DPO objective addresses glyph-level errors better than standard DPO.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Region-level preference optimization improves glyph accuracy over standard DPO
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: R-GDPO substantially enhances accuracy; Regional Reward Guidance enables control

title: Tri-Prompting: Video Diffusion with Unified Control over Scene, Subject, and Motion
task: Unified video generation with joint scene, subject, and motion control via Tri-Prompting
key_results: Superior multi-view subject identity and 3D consistency; unified framework supporting novel workflows (3D-aware subject insertion, subject manipulation)
comments: Elegant three-stage control via dual-condition motion module; supports practical creative workflows.

model_io.model: Tri-Prompting (video diffusion with 3D tracking + RGB conditions)
outputs: video with consistent subjects and motion

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified framework can jointly control scene, subject, and motion in video generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: superior multi-view consistency; enables novel creative workflows

title: Spectrum Matching: a Unified Perspective for Superior Diffusability in Latent Diffusion
task: Improving discrete image generation via spectrum matching of latent encodings
key_results: Superior generation on CelebA and ImageNet; outperforms prior approaches; unified view clarifying prior observations (over-noisy/smooth latents)
comments: Principled spectral approach to VAE diffusability; explains and improves upon prior methods.

model_io.model: Latent diffusion with Spectrum Matching (ESM + DSM)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flattened power-law spectrum in latent space improves diffusability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: ESM and DSM enhance generation quality; unifies prior glyph observations

title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism
task: Unified KV cache management for efficient multi-task parallel inference in vision-language-action models
key_results: Up to 3.7x speedup; 200+ tokens/s language throughput + 70 Hz action frequency simultaneously without quality degradation
comments: Practical system contribution; unified KV cache abstraction enables cross-task sharing.

model_io.model: OxyGen (unified KV cache manager for MoT VLAs)
outputs: parallel language generation and action commands

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified KV cache enables efficient cross-task sharing and continuous batching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 3.7x speedup; simultaneous 200 tokens/s language and 70 Hz action generation

title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
task: Generalizable dynamic manipulation via dataset and dynamics-aware VLA architecture
key_results: PUMA achieves 6.3% absolute improvement in success rate; dynamic data improves robustness to static tasks; 35 tasks with 110K trajectories
comments: First large-scale dynamic manipulation benchmark; historical optical flow + world queries for spatiotemporal reasoning.

inputs: image history with optical flow, task description

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit historical and predictive spatiotemporal reasoning improves dynamic manipulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 6.3% improvement over baselines; dynamic data transfers to static tasks

title: Efficient Document Parsing via Parallel Token Prediction
task: Accelerating document parsing via parallel token prediction in vision-language models
key_results: 1.6x-2.2x speedup; reduced hallucinations; strong generalization; comprehensive data generation pipeline
comments: Model-agnostic method; practical speedup for document understanding without quality loss.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable tokens with parallel decoding objectives accelerate document parsing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 1.6x-2.2x speedup with reduced hallucinations and good generalization

title: Mind the Shift: Decoding Monetary Policy Stance from FOMC Statements with Large Language Models
task: Extracting monetary policy stance from FOMC statements via LLM representations and self-supervision
key_results: 71.1% sentence-level classification accuracy; correlation with inflation indicators; association with Treasury yield movements
comments: Novel application of CCA and relative temporal structure; economically meaningful without manual labels.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM representations encode monetary-policy signals recoverable via relative temporal structure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 71.1% accuracy; scores correlate with inflation and yield movements

title: Autonomous Agents Coordinating Distributed Discovery Through Emergent Artifact Exchange
task: Autonomous scientific investigation via distributed agents with emergent artifact exchange
key_results: 4 autonomous investigations (peptide design, ceramics, interdisciplinary bridging, analogies); agents discover needs and synthesize across analyses
comments: Ambitious framework for multi-agent scientific discovery; provenance-aware governance and autonomous mutation are sophisticated.

outputs: artifacts with lineage, auditable scientific findings

hypotheses.hypothesis: Independent agents coordinating via artifact exchange enable complex multi-domain scientific discovery
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 4 successful investigations show heterogeneous tool chaining and emergent convergence

title: MoKus: Leveraging Cross-Modal Knowledge Transfer for Knowledge-Aware Concept Customization
task: Knowledge-aware visual concept customization with cross-modal knowledge transfer
key_results: MoKus outperforms SOTA; enables virtual concept creation and concept erasure; improves world knowledge benchmarks
comments: Novel insight on cross-modal knowledge transfer; introduces knowledge-aware task and KnowCusBench.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-modal knowledge transfer enables binding diverse textual knowledge to visual concepts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms SOTA; supports virtual creation and erasure with knowledge preservation

title: Garments2Look: A Multi-Reference Dataset for High-Fidelity Outfit-Level Virtual Try-On with Clothing and Accessories
task: Multi-reference dataset and evaluation for outfit-level virtual try-on with accessories and layering
key_results: 80K many-garments-to-look pairs across 40 major and 300+ fine-grained categories; baselines struggle with seamless try-on and layering
comments: First outfit-level VTON benchmark; synthesis pipeline with quality assurance is rigorous.

model_io.model: VTON methods (adapted SOTA and general editing models)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Outfit-level VTON requires coordinated multi-garment and accessory reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: current methods struggle with layering and styling coordination

title: SCoCCA: Multi-modal Sparse Concept Decomposition via Canonical Correlation Analysis
task: Interpreting vision-language models via multi-modal sparse concept decomposition and CCA
key_results: SCoCCA achieves SOTA concept discovery; enables improved activation, ablation, and semantic manipulation; addresses modality gap
comments: Principled CCA-based approach to multi-modal interpretability; sparse concepts more disentangled.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCA can align cross-modal embeddings while enabling sparse concept discovery
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SOTA concept discovery; improved ablation and manipulation over baselines

title: VoXtream2: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking rate control
task: Full-stream text-to-speech with dynamic speaking rate control via duration distribution matching
key_results: Competitive zero-shot performance; 4x faster than real-time with 74 ms first-packet latency; dynamic rate control mid-utterance
comments: Practical streaming TTS with no prompt transcription required; classifier-free guidance improves control.

inputs: text, speaker prompt, optional rate parameter
outputs: audio stream with controllable speaking rate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distribution matching over duration states enables controllable streaming TTS
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: competitive performance with dynamic rate control and minimal latency

title: SNCE: Geometry-Aware Supervision for Scalable Discrete Image Generation
task: Improving large-codebook discrete image generation via geometric soft-supervision in latent space
key_results: SNCE improves convergence speed and generation quality vs standard cross-entropy; SOTA on ImageNet, text-to-image, and editing
comments: Elegant geometric approach to codebook supervision; captures semantic structure in quantization.

model_io.model: Discrete image generator with SNCE objective
outputs: reconstructed/generated image with large VQ codebook

hypotheses.hypothesis: Soft supervision via neighbor proximity improves large-codebook generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: faster convergence, better quality; SOTA on diverse generation tasks

title: sebis at ArchEHR-QA 2026: How Much Can You Do Locally? Evaluating Grounded EHR QA on a Single Notebook
task: Grounded EHR question answering on local hardware without cloud infrastructure
key_results: Competitive performance on ArchEHR-QA leaderboards on commodity hardware; above-average on 2/4 subtasks; smaller models approach large system performance
comments: Privacy-preserving EHR QA is feasible locally; practical for clinical deployment constraints.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Privacy-preserving EHR QA is feasible with local models on commodity hardware
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: competitive leaderboard performance without external APIs; practical for clinics

title: Structures of respiratory supercomplexes and ATP synthase oligomers in mammalian mitochondrial inner membrane
task: Determine high-resolution structures of ATP synthase and respiratory supercomplexes in native mitochondrial membranes
key_results: Resolved FoF1 ATP synthase as dimers and tetramers stabilized by IF₁; identified new respiratory supercomplex compositions including CI₁CIII₂CIV₃ and CI₂CIII₂CIV₆ mega-complex
comments: Cryo-EM on native SMPs elegantly bridges structural and functional studies, though the absence of discrete lipid density in the c₈-ring warrants further investigation.

outputs: 3D structures of FoF1 dimers and tetramers
electron density maps of Fo domain and e-subunit–c₈-ring interface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tetrameric FoF1 units contribute to cristae tip shaping in mammalian mitochondria
hypotheses.result: Tetrameric assemblies of FoF1–IF₁ dimers identified in native membrane

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multiple respiratory supercomplex compositions exist in mammalian mitochondria
hypotheses.result: Identified previously unreported CI₁CIII₂CIV₃ and CI₂CIII₂CIV₆ assemblies alongside known compositions

title: Massively parallel quantification of mutational impact on IAPP amyloid formation
task: Map mutational effects on IAPP amyloid nucleation across 1916 variants
key_results: Residues 15-32 are mutation-sensitive with core constraints at 21-27; nucleation-slowing mutations correlate between IAPP and Aβ, but nucleation-accelerating mutations do not transfer predictively
comments: Deep mutational scanning provides unprecedented coverage, but the lack of predictive transfer of acceleration mutations between amyloids suggests distinct nucleation mechanisms despite sequence conservation.

inputs: islet amyloid polypeptide variants (substitutions, insertions, truncations, deletions)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Residues 15-32 form a structured core in amyloid fibrils and early aggregates
hypotheses.result: This region shows highest sensitivity to mutation and matches in vitro nucleation intermediates

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nucleation mechanisms are conserved between IAPP and Aβ amyloids
hypotheses.result: Only mutations that slow nucleation correlate; acceleration mutations are amyloid-specific

title: Kinase KEY1 controls pyrenoid condensate size throughout the cell cycle by disrupting phase separation interactions
task: Elucidate kinase-mediated regulation of pyrenoid condensate size and dynamics during cell cycle
key_results: KEY1 kinase regulates pyrenoid size by phosphorylating EPYC1 linker protein to disrupt Rubisco–EPYC1 interactions; key1 mutants exhibit multiple small condensates that fail to dissolve
comments: The mechanistic model elegantly connects kinase activity to phase separation control, though the regulatory factors determining condensate position and the role of additional post-translational modifications remain to be explored.

outputs: temporal dynamics of size and number

hypotheses.hypothesis: KEY1 regulates pyrenoid size by disrupting phase separation via EPYC1 phosphorylation
hypotheses.result: KEY1 localizes to condensates and phosphorylates EPYC1 to weaken Rubisco–EPYC1 interactions, promoting dissolution

hypotheses.hypothesis: KEY1 is necessary for normal pyrenoid dissolution during cell division
hypotheses.result: key1 mutants retain multiple smaller condensates throughout cell cycle and fail to dissolve during division

title: Polar chromosomes are rescued from missegregation by spindle elongation-driven microtubule pivoting
task: Elucidate mechanism of polar chromosome rescue from missegregation
key_results: Spindle elongation drives astral microtubule pivoting around centrosome repositioning polar chromosomes; Eg5/KIF11 activity determines pivoting direction and extent; polar chromosomes form mono-lateral attachments lacking Astrin
comments: The pivoting mechanism provides an elegant solution to chromosome congression errors, though the energetic cost and timing constraints of this rescue process warrant further investigation.

model_io.model: mammalian cell mitosis with polar chromosome positioning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spindle elongation rescues polar chromosomes from behind the pole via microtubule pivoting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enhanced Eg5 activity accelerates polar chromosome resolution

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mono-lateral attachments on polar chromosomes differ from bi-lateral attachments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced Astrin recruitment and partial Mad2 localization observed

title: Phosphoregulation of the novel hemi-arrestin MAPK scaffold Sms1 prevents untimely mating
task: Identify and characterize MAPK scaffold regulation in yeast sexual differentiation
key_results: Sms1 hemi-arrestin protein scaffolds MAPK cascade at PIP2-rich plasma membrane patches; phosphorylation by MAPK creates negative feedback suppressing membrane translocation; polarized growth and signal localization require Sms1-kinase interactions
comments: The convergent evolution of MAPK scaffolds across structural folds suggests functional constraints that transcend protein architecture, implying robust signaling design principles.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sms1 functions as essential MAPK cascade scaffold despite hemi-arrestin fold
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Sms1 recruits and activates each MAP kinase in cascade

hypotheses.hypothesis: MAPK-mediated phosphorylation of Sms1 creates negative feedback
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phosphorylation antagonizes membrane translocation preventing untimely mating

title: Calcium-mediated calreticulin-IRE1α interaction drives dynamic fluctuation of IRE1α activity under chronic endoplasmic reticulum stress
task: Characterize IRE1α activity dynamics during chronic ER stress
key_results: IRE1α exhibits "up-down-up" fluctuation pattern; calreticulin-IRE1α interaction inversely correlates with Ca²⁺ concentration showing "down-up-down" pattern; circadian oscillation of IRE1α phosphorylation observed in mouse liver
comments: The calcium-mediated mechanism elegantly explains how cells sustain intermediate UPR signaling during prolonged stress, though the functional consequences of these oscillations on cell fate remain to be fully elucidated.

model_io.model: pharmacologically-induced chronic ER stress in cultured cells and mouse liver

hypotheses.hypothesis: Calcium depletion reduces calreticulin-IRE1α binding, allowing IRE1α activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Ca²⁺ concentration negatively correlates with interaction intensity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Circadian regulation modulates ER stress responses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; rhythmic oscillation of calreticulin-IRE1α interaction in mouse liver

title: β-tubulin phosphorylation by Chk1 is required for normal spindle formation during cell division
task: Determine role of Chk1-mediated β-tubulin phosphorylation in spindle assembly
key_results: Chk1 phosphorylates β-tubulin-T285 at mitotic centrosomes; impaired phosphorylation causes reduced spindle density, delayed anaphase, chromosome misalignment, and unequal cell division; ATR-ATRIP-TopBP1 pathway recruits Chk1 to centrosomes
comments: The coupling of DNA damage checkpoint kinases to normal mitotic spindle assembly suggests evolutionary conservation of checkpoint mechanisms even under unperturbed conditions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chk1 is required for optimal spindle assembly during normal mitosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Chk1 knockout and β-tubulin-T285 mutations impair spindle function

hypotheses.hypothesis: ATR pathway promotes Chk1 centrosomal localization during mitosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ATRIP-ATR-TopBP1 interaction essential for Chk1 activation at centrosomes

title: Local lateral connectivity is sufficient for replicating cortex-like topographical organization in deep neural networks
task: Learning topographic organization in visual cortex models via lateral connectivity
key_results: Deep neural networks with local lateral connections spontaneously develop cortex-like topographic maps across visual hierarchy without explicit topography objectives; improved adversarial robustness
comments: Emergent organization without inductive bias is compelling, but the relationship between lateral connectivity strength and map robustness remains underexplored.

model_io.model: Deep neural networks with local lateral connections

hypotheses.hypothesis: Local lateral connections alone drive cortex-like topographic map formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: modular organization emerges without explicit topography-inducing objectives

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lateral connectivity improves adversarial robustness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: networks with lateral connections show improved robustness to adversarial perturbations

title: VISDB 2.0: A manually curated resource of viral integration sites and their regulatory maps in human diseases
task: Curating and annotating human viral integration sites
key_results: 270,000+ manually curated VISs from 209 publications covering 11 viruses; 3.5-fold increase over previous version with standardized genomic coordinates and regulatory annotations
comments: Comprehensive resource but relies on manual curation which may introduce inconsistencies; limited to published data and subject to publication bias.

title: An attention-based multimodal deep learning framework integrating EEG and ECG for enhanced stress detection
task: Multimodal stress detection integrating EEG and ECG via attention fusion
key_results: 95.7% accuracy in stress classification (3-way: neutral/tense/positive) using EEG+ECG fusion vs 82.3% EEG-only and 85.6% ECG-only; validated on WESAD and CASE datasets
comments: Strong improvement from multimodal fusion and attention mechanisms, but limited to two datasets and modest sample size (35 subjects per dataset); generalization to diverse populations unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multimodal EEG+ECG fusion improves stress classification over unimodal approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 95.7% accuracy vs 82.3% (EEG) and 85.6% (ECG)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Attention-weighted fusion effectively combines complementary EEG and ECG signals
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: EEG and ECG signals enhance each other's stress assessment capability

title: Biosynthesis of pyomelanin from methanol with engineered Komagataella phaffii and its characterizations
task: Engineering high-yield pyomelanin biosynthesis from methanol
key_results: 66-fold increase in homogentisic acid yield through balancing three biosynthetic modules; final strain Pyo29 achieved 70.5 ± 0.7 g/L pyomelanin titer after 204 h fermentation in 5-L fermenter
comments: The engineering strategy elegantly combines directed evolution and rational design with a clever color-screening system, though the mechanism for comparing oxidative polymerization versus laccase-catalyzed synthesis could be more mechanistically detailed.

model_io.model: Engineered K. phaffii metabolic pathway for pyomelanin production

hypotheses.hypothesis: Balancing three biosynthetic modules increases precursor yield
hypotheses.result: 66-fold increase in homogentisic acid titer achieved through module optimization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Directed evolution and semirational design improve pathway enzyme activity
hypotheses.result: Enhanced 3-deoxy-D-arabinoheptulosonate 7-phosphate synthase and hydroxyphenylpyruvate dioxygenase variants improved flux

title: Origins and breadth of pairwise epistasis in an α-helix of β-lactamase TEM-1
task: Characterizing mechanistic basis of epistasis in protein evolution
key_results: Comprehensive analysis of 14,000+ double mutants in TEM-1 β-lactamase α-helix; two-state stability model predicts ~80% of epistatic effects; systematic deviations occur only when residues directly interact in 3D structure
comments: The finding that most epistasis is predictable from additive single-mutation effects is encouraging for evolution modeling, but the structural exceptions suggest context-dependent effects remain difficult to generalize across proteins.

model_io.model: Two-state protein stability model with additive contributions
Statistical models trained on homologous sequences
inputs: Sequence alignments from distant TEM-1 homologs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Destabilizing/neutral/stabilizing mutations contribute additively to stability phenotype
hypotheses.result: Model explains most epistatic effects consistently across fitness and MIC measures

hypotheses.hypothesis: Direct 3D structural interactions cause deviations from additive model
hypotheses.result: Systematic deviations occur specifically at residue pairs with direct contacts in folded structure
